title: Iran Conflict Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Day 23 of the Iran-US/Israel conflict. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi formally rejected all ceasefire negotiations, stating Tehran never sought a ceasefire and holds Washington responsible for the war. The Strait of Hormuz crisis deepened with 20 million barrels per day backed up after IRGC threatened to block all oil transit, representing the largest energy supply disruption since the 1970s. Oman, Qatar, and China have positioned themselves as potential back-channel mediators. US Navy announced tanker escort operations in the region.

title: Iran Conflict Update — Mar 21, 2026
summary: New diplomatic exchanges reported between Tehran and Washington via Omani intermediaries. IAEA inspectors confirmed enrichment levels at Fordow remain at 60%, unchanged from previous assessment. Updated map data reflects repositioning of naval assets in the Strait of Hormuz following increased tanker escort operations. Economic indicators show continued pressure on Iranian rial with parallel market rate declining 3% week-over-week.

title: Iran Conflict Update — Mar 20, 2026
summary: Israeli defense officials confirmed completion of Arrow-4 interceptor deployment along northern border. Three new map lines added tracking reported drone incursions from Iraqi militia positions. Casualty data updated with UNHCR revised displacement figures for western Iran border regions. Political section updated with new statements from EU foreign policy chief regarding sanctions review timeline.

title: Iran Conflict Update — Mar 18, 2026
summary: CENTCOM reported intercept of two one-way attack drones over northeastern Syria attributed to Iran-backed groups. Updated strike targets data with new satellite imagery analysis of Parchin military complex. KPI dashboard refreshed with latest IAEA safeguards report data showing 12 cascades operational at Natanz. Claims matrix updated with competing narratives on Red Sea shipping disruption responsibility.
